text: The first space feels serene and almost void; the emptiness and softness of the beanbags invite introspection and contemplation. The starkness of the white walls amplifies my thoughts, and I am aware of the potential for creativity or loneliness here. The second, filled with color and screens, stimulates my curiosity. The presence of R+D and the entanglement of words and images provoke thoughts about the interface between humanity and technology, making me reflect on how we continuously redefine ourselves through innovation. There’s a quiet excitement here—the intersection of comfort and inquiry feels profoundly philosophical.
